title: Isodon oreophilus
taxonomy: Isodon oreophilus is a species of plant in the family Lamiaceae. It belongs to the genus Isodon, which is a group of flowering plants commonly known as Chinese sage or Chinese mint. The species Isodon oreophilus is closely related to other members of the Lamiaceae family, which includes plants such as basil, mint, and rosemary. Within the Lamiaceae family, Isodon oreophilus is classified as a member of the subfamily Lamioideae. This classification is based on the plant's morphology and its evolutionary relationships with other members of the Lamiaceae family.

It is sometimes used to treat various health issues, including fever, rheumatism, and digestive problems.
